title: Brown Planthopper Detected in North Field
description: Satellite imagery analysis from Oct 5 reveals characteristic stippling damage patterns in Zone Z1 (North Field), consistent with Brown Planthopper (Nilaparvata lugens) infestation at early stage. Affected area estimated at 0.3 ha.
recommended_action: Apply imidacloprid 17.8% SL at 125 ml/ha. Drain field for 3 days before application. Monitor neighboring zones Z2 and Z3 closely.
sms_template: ALERT: Brown Planthopper detected in North Field. Apply imidacloprid 125ml/ha. Drain field 3 days. Contact: 1800-XXX for help.

title: Soil Moisture Critical — Irrigation Required Within 6 Hours
description: Time-series model predicts soil moisture in Blocks A and C will drop below wilting point (30%) within 6 hours based on current ET0 of 6.8 mm/day and 0 mm precipitation forecast for the next 5 days.
recommended_action: Irrigate Blocks A and C immediately with 40 mm application. Schedule Block B for irrigation within 18 hours. Consider mulching to reduce ET losses.
sms_template: URGENT: Soil moisture critically low on Obi Chukwu Farm. Irrigate Block A & C NOW with 40mm. Block B within 18hrs. Drought risk: HIGH.

title: Optimal Harvest Window: 18–22 October
description: Based on NDVI trajectory (0.84 current), weather forecast (low rain from Oct 10–14), and historical brix development data for Arka Rakshak variety, the optimal harvest window for maximum marketable yield is October 18–22.
recommended_action: Arrange transport and market contacts by Oct 17. Reduce irrigation 5 days before harvest. Harvest early morning (before 9 AM) to maintain quality.
sms_template: Tomato harvest window: Oct 18-22. Stop irrigation 5 days before. Harvest before 9AM for best quality. Arrange market contact now.

title: Severe Drought Risk — 91/100
description: Drought risk model scores 91/100. North Plot has reached critical stress threshold with 18% soil moisture. Current conditions mirror the 2023 drought event that caused 60% yield loss. ET0 exceeds field capacity replenishment by 7.8 mm/day.
recommended_action: Emergency irrigation required on North Plot. Apply 60 mm in two split doses (30mm today, 30mm tomorrow). Contact district water board for emergency allocation. Consider crop insurance claim.
sms_template: CRITICAL DROUGHT ALERT: Abubakar Farm North Plot at 18% moisture. Apply 60mm irrigation NOW in 2 doses. Contact water board urgently.

title: Pink Bollworm Trap Count Elevated in Block 3
description: Pheromone trap image analysis shows pink bollworm (Pectinophora gossypiella) adult count at 8/trap/week in Block 3, approaching economic threshold of 10/trap/week. Early detection allows preventive action.
recommended_action: Deploy mating disruption dispensers at 100/ha in Block 3. Apply emamectin benzoate 1.9% EC if trap count exceeds 10 next week. Inspect bolls for internal damage.
sms_template: Pink bollworm count rising in Block 3 (8/trap). Deploy mating dispensers. Spray emamectin if count >10 next week. Check bolls for damage.

title: East Field Over-Irrigation Risk
description: Zone Z3 (East Field) shows soil moisture at 81%, significantly above optimal range for paddy at grain filling stage (65–75%). Continued irrigation may cause waterlogging, increasing risk of root rot and blast disease.
recommended_action: Skip next scheduled irrigation for Zone Z3. Install drainage channel between Z3 and Z4 before next rain event. Monitor for blast symptoms over next 5 days.
sms_template: East Field too wet (81%). Skip next irrigation for this zone. Add drainage channel. Watch for blast disease signs over next 5 days.

title: NDVI Decline in Block B — Nutrient Deficiency Suspected
description: Multispectral analysis shows 12% NDVI decline in Block B over 14 days. Spectral signature matches nitrogen deficiency pattern. Crop growth stage (V8) makes this a critical intervention window.
recommended_action: Apply top-dress nitrogen at 50 kg/ha urea within 3 days. Conduct soil test for phosphorus and potassium levels. Consider foliar micro-nutrient spray (zinc sulphate 0.5%).
sms_template: NDVI falling in Block B — likely N deficiency. Apply 50kg/ha urea in 3 days. Also spray zinc sulphate 0.5%. Soil test recommended.

title: Fungal Disease Risk Elevated Post Rain
description: 7-day rain forecast (cumulative 37 mm) combined with high relative humidity (>85%) creates elevated risk for early blight (Alternaria solani) in tomato crop. Risk window: Oct 7–10.
recommended_action: Apply mancozeb 75 WP at 2.5 g/L as protective spray on Oct 8 before predicted rain. Ensure good air circulation in Greenhouse blocks. Avoid overhead irrigation.
sms_template: Fungal risk high (Oct 7-10). Spray mancozeb 2.5g/L on Oct 8. Avoid overhead irrigation. Ensure good airflow in greenhouse.

title: Downi Mildew Risk During Expected Recovery Rain
description: After predicted thunderstorm on Oct 10 (28 mm), rapid soil moisture increase followed by hot drying conditions creates high downy mildew risk for pearl millet. Historical loss from this pattern: 25–40%.
recommended_action: Apply metalaxyl-M + mancozeb (Ridomil Gold) at 2.5 kg/ha immediately after rain. Target application within 24 hours of rainfall event.
sms_template: ALERT: After Oct 10 rain, spray Ridomil Gold 2.5kg/ha within 24hrs. Downy mildew risk very HIGH. Act fast to protect yield.

title: Irrigation Schedule Optimized for October
description: Time-series model projects optimal irrigation schedule for cotton bolling stage: irrigate every 5 days with 35 mm application. Current 5-day forecast includes 18 mm rain, reducing irrigation need by approximately 50% this week.
recommended_action: Reduce irrigation application to 15 mm (instead of 35 mm) this week due to forecast rain. Maintain 5-day schedule otherwise. Check furrow depth before next irrigation.
sms_template: Reduce irrigation this week to 15mm (rain expected: 18mm). Normal 35mm next week. Check furrow depth before irrigation.
